  [user_task_description] # Requirements Document for 'JobBoard' Web Application  ## 1. Objective Develop a comprehensive web application named 'JobBoard' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to browse job postings, submit resumes, track applications, view company profiles, and manage job applications. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.  ## 2. Language The required development language for the 'JobBoard' application is Python.  ## 3. Page Design  The 'JobBoard' web application will consist of the following nine pages:  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Page Title**: Job Board Dashboard - **Overview**: The main hub displaying featured job postings, latest opportunities, and quick navigation to all functionalities. - **Elements**:   - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.   - **ID: featured-jobs** - Type: Div - Display of featured job recommendations.   - **ID: browse-jobs-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to job listings page.   - **ID: my-applications-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to applications tracking page.   - **ID: companies-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to companies directory page.  ### 2. Job Listings Page - **Page Title**: Job Listings - **Overview**: A page displaying all available job postings with search and filter capabilities. - **Elements**:   - **ID: listings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the listings page.   - **ID: search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search jobs by title, company, or location.   - **ID: category-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by job category (Technology, Finance, Healthcare, etc.).   - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location (Remote, On-site, Hybrid).   - **ID: jobs-grid** - Type: Div - Grid displaying job cards with title, company, location, and salary range.   - **ID: view-job-button-{job_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view job details (each job card has this).  ### 3. Job Details Page - **Page Title**: Job Details - **Overview**: A page displaying detailed information about a specific job posting. - **Elements**:   - **ID: job-details-page** - Type: Div - Container for the job details page.   - **ID: job-title** - Type: H1 - Display job title.   - **ID: company-name** - Type: Div - Display company name.   - **ID: job-description** - Type: Div - Display full job description and requirements.   - **ID: salary-range** - Type: Div - Display salary range.   - **ID: apply-now-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply for the job.  ### 4. Application Form Page - **Page Title**: Submit Application - **Overview**: A page for users to submit job applications with resume and cover letter. - **Elements**:   - **ID: application-form-page** - Type: Div - Container for the application form page.   - **ID: applicant-name** - Type: Input - Field to input applicant name.   - **ID: applicant-email** - Type: Input - Field to input applicant email.   - **ID: resume-upload** - Type: File Input - Field to upload resume file.   - **ID: cover-letter** - Type: Textarea - Field to enter cover letter text.   - **ID: submit-application-button** - Type: Button - Button to submit application.  ### 5. Application Tracking Page - **Page Title**: My Applications - **Overview**: A page displaying all submitted applications with status tracking. - **Elements**:   - **ID: tracking-page** - Type: Div - Container for the tracking page.   - **ID: applications-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying applications with job title, company, status, and date applied.   - **ID: status-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by status (All, Applied, Under Review, Interview, Rejected).   - **ID: view-application-button-{app_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view application details (each application has this).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 6. Companies Directory Page - **Page Title**: Company Directory - **Overview**: A page displaying all registered companies with their profiles and available jobs. - **Elements**:   - **ID: companies-page** - Type: Div - Container for the companies page.   - **ID: companies-list** - Type: Div - List of company cards with company name, industry, and employee count.   - **ID: search-company-input** - Type: Input - Field to search companies by name or industry.   - **ID: view-company-button-{company_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view company profile (each company card has this).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 7. Company Profile Page - **Page Title**: Company Profile - **Overview**: A page displaying detailed information about a specific company. - **Elements**:   - **ID: company-profile-page** - Type: Div - Container for the company profile page.   - **ID: company-info** - Type: Div - Display company name, industry, location, and description.   - **ID: company-jobs** - Type: Div - Display all open jobs from this company.   - **ID: jobs-list** - Type: Div - List of jobs with titles and status indicators.   - **ID: view-job-button-{job_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view job details from company profile.   - **ID: back-to-companies** - Type: Button - Button to go back to companies directory.  ### 8. Resume Management Page - **Page Title**: My Resumes - **Overview**: A page for users to upload and manage multiple resumes. - **Elements**:   - **ID: resume-page** - Type: Div - Container for the resume page.   - **ID: resumes-list** - Type: Div - List of uploaded resumes with upload date.   - **ID: upload-resume-button** - Type: Button - Button to upload a new resume.   - **ID: resume-file-input** - Type: File Input - Hidden file input for resume upload.   - **ID: delete-resume-button-{resume_id}** - Type: Button - Button to delete a resume (each resume has this).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 9. Search Results Page - **Page Title**: Search Results - **Overview**: A page displaying search results for jobs and companies. - **Elements**:   - **ID: search-results-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search results page.   - **ID: search-query-display** - Type: Div - Display the search query entered.   - **ID: results-tabs** - Type: Div - Tabs to switch between job results and company results.   - **ID: job-results** - Type: Div - Display search results for jobs.   - **ID: company-results** - Type: Div - Display search results for companies.   - **ID: no-results-message** - Type: Div - Display when no results are found.  ## 4. Data Storage  The 'JobBoard' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. Jobs Data - **File Name**: `jobs.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   job_id|title|company_id|location|salary_min|salary_max|category|description|posted_date   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15   2|Data Analyst|2|New York, NY|60000|85000|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16   3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles, CA|50000|70000|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14   ```  ### 2. Companies Data - **File Name**: `companies.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   company_id|company_name|industry|location|employee_count|description   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider   2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York, NY|300|Innovative financial services company   3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles, CA|200|Premier healthcare management organization   ```  ### 3. Categories Data - **File Name**: `categories.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   category_id|category_name|description   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions   2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions   3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions   ```  ### 4. Applications Data - **File Name**: `applications.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   application_id|job_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|status|applied_date|resume_id   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1   2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2   3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3   ```  ### 5. Resumes Data - **File Name**: `resumes.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   resume_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|filename|upload_date|summary   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience   2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background   3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience   ```  ### 6. Job Categories Mapping Data - **File Name**: `job_categories.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   mapping_id|job_id|category_id   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|1   2|2|2   3|3|3   ```

Container for resume page\n  - resumes-list (Div): List of uploaded resumes\n  - upload-resume-button (Button): Button to upload new resume\n  - resume-file-input (File Input, hidden): Hidden file input for upload\n  - delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }} (Button): Delete resume button for each resume\n  - back-to-dashboard (Button): Navigate back to dashboard\n- Context Variables:\n  - resumes: list of dicts: resume_id (int), applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), filename (str), upload_date (str), summary (str)\n- Navigation Mappings:\n  - upload-resume-button: triggers hidden resume-file-input's file selection\n  - delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }}: posts to /delete_resume/{{ resume.resume_id }}\n  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard')\n\n### 9. templates/search_results.html\n- Page Title: Search Results\n- Element IDs:\n  - search-results-page (Div): Container for search results page\n  - search-query-display (Div): Display the entered search query\n  - results-tabs (Div): Tabs to switch between job and company results\n  - job-results (Div): Displays job search results\n  - company-results (Div): Displays company search results\n  - no-results-message (Div): Shows message if no results\n- Context Variables:\n  - query: str - The search query text\n  - job_results: list of dicts: job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str)\n  - company_results: list of dicts: company_id (int), company_name (str), industry (str)\n- Navigation Mappings:\n  - Buttons or links to job details: url_for('job_details', job_id=job.job_id)\n  - Buttons or links to company profiles: url_for('company_profile', company_id=company.company_id)\n\n---\n\n## Section 3: Data File Schemas\n\n### 1. data/jobs.txt\n- Pipe-Delimited Fields:\n  1. job_id (int) - Unique identifier for the job\n  2. title (str) - Job title\n  3. company_id (int) - ID of the company offering the job\n  4. location (str) - Job location\n  5. salary_min (int) - Minimum salary offered\n  6. salary_max (int) - Maximum salary offered\n  7. category (str) - Job category\n  8. description (str) - Full job description and requirements\n  9. posted_date (str, YYYY-MM-DD) - Date the job was posted\n- Description: Stores all job postings with details\n- Example Rows:\n```\n1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15\n2|Data Analyst|2|New York, NY|60000|85000|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16\n3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles, CA|50000|70000|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14\n```\n\n### 2. data/companies.txt\n- Pipe-Delimited Fields:\n  1. company_id (int) - Unique identifier for the company\n  2. company_name (str) - Name of the company\n  3. industry (str) - Industry sector\n  4. location (str) - Company location\n  5. employee_count (int) - Number of employees\n  6. description (str) - Company description\n- Description: Stores all registered companies and their profiles\n- Example Rows:\n```\n1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider\n2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York, NY|300|Innovative financial services company\n3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles, CA|200|Premier healthcare management organization\n```\n\n### 3. data/categories.txt\n- Pipe-Delimited Fields:\n  1. category_id (int) - Unique identifier for the category\n  2. category_name (str) - Name of the category\n  3. description (str) - Description of the category\n- Description: Stores job categories definitions\n- Example Rows:\n```\n1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions\n2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions\n3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions\n```\n\n### 4. data/applications.txt\n- Pipe-Delimited Fields:\n  1. application_id (int) - Unique identifier for the application\n  2. job_id (int) - ID of the job applied for\n  3. applicant_name (str) - Name of the applicant\n  4. applicant_email (str) - Email of the applicant\n  5. status (str) - Current application status\n  6. applied_date (str, YYYY-MM-DD) - Date applied\n  7. resume_id (int) - ID for the uploaded resume used\n- Description: Stores all job applications submitted\n- Example Rows:\n```\n1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1\n2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2\n3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3\n```\n\n### 5. data/resumes.txt\n- Pipe-Delimited Fields:\n  1. resume_id (int) - Unique identifier for the resume\n  2. applicant_name (str) - Name of the applicant\n  3. applicant_email (str) - Email of the applicant\n  4. filename (str) - Filename of the uploaded resume\n  5. upload_date (str, YYYY-MM-DD) - Date uploaded\n  6. summary (str) - Short professional summary\n- Description: Stores resumes uploaded by users\n- Example Rows:\n```\n1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience\n2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background\n3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience\n```\n\n### 6. data/job_categories.txt\n- Pipe-Delimited Fields:\n  1. mapping_id (int) - Unique identifier for mapping\n  2. job_id (int) - Job ID\n  3. category_id (int) - Category ID\n- Description: Maps jobs to categories\n- Example Rows:\n```\n1|1|1\n2|2|2\n3|3|3\n```\n\n---\n\nThis completes the comprehensive design specification document for the JobBoard web application, enabling Backend and Frontend developers to implement the project independently and in coordination.\n"})

[DEBUG] Review Content for SystemArchitect -> BackendDeveloper:
  [user_task_description] # Requirements Document for 'JobBoard' Web Application  ## 1. Objective Develop a comprehensive web application named 'JobBoard' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to browse job postings, submit resumes, track applications, view company profiles, and manage job applications. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.  ## 2. Language The required development language for the 'JobBoard' application is Python.  ## 3. Page Design  The 'JobBoard' web application will consist of the following nine pages:  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Page Title**: Job Board Dashboard - **Overview**: The main hub displaying featured job postings, latest opportunities, and quick navigation to all functionalities. - **Elements**:   - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.   - **ID: featured-jobs** - Type: Div - Display of featured job recommendations.   - **ID: browse-jobs-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to job listings page.   - **ID: my-applications-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to applications tracking page.   - **ID: companies-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to companies directory page.  ### 2. Job Listings Page - **Page Title**: Job Listings - **Overview**: A page displaying all available job postings with search and filter capabilities. - **Elements**:   - **ID: listings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the listings page.   - **ID: search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search jobs by title, company, or location.   - **ID: category-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by job category (Technology, Finance, Healthcare, etc.).   - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location (Remote, On-site, Hybrid).   - **ID: jobs-grid** - Type: Div - Grid displaying job cards with title, company, location, and salary range.   - **ID: view-job-button-{job_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view job details (each job card has this).  ### 3. Job Details Page - **Page Title**: Job Details - **Overview**: A page displaying detailed information about a specific job posting. - **Elements**:   - **ID: job-details-page** - Type: Div - Container for the job details page.   - **ID: job-title** - Type: H1 - Display job title.   - **ID: company-name** - Type: Div - Display company name.   - **ID: job-description** - Type: Div - Display full job description and requirements.   - **ID: salary-range** - Type: Div - Display salary range.   - **ID: apply-now-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply for the job.  ### 4. Application Form Page - **Page Title**: Submit Application - **Overview**: A page for users to submit job applications with resume and cover letter. - **Elements**:   - **ID: application-form-page** - Type: Div - Container for the application form page.   - **ID: applicant-name** - Type: Input - Field to input applicant name.   - **ID: applicant-email** - Type: Input - Field to input applicant email.   - **ID: resume-upload** - Type: File Input - Field to upload resume file.   - **ID: cover-letter** - Type: Textarea - Field to enter cover letter text.   - **ID: submit-application-button** - Type: Button - Button to submit application.  ### 5. Application Tracking Page - **Page Title**: My Applications - **Overview**: A page displaying all submitted applications with status tracking. - **Elements**:   - **ID: tracking-page** - Type: Div - Container for the tracking page.   - **ID: applications-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying applications with job title, company, status, and date applied.   - **ID: status-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by status (All, Applied, Under Review, Interview, Rejected).   - **ID: view-application-button-{app_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view application details (each application has this).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 6. Companies Directory Page - **Page Title**: Company Directory - **Overview**: A page displaying all registered companies with their profiles and available jobs. - **Elements**:   - **ID: companies-page** - Type: Div - Container for the companies page.   - **ID: companies-list** - Type: Div - List of company cards with company name, industry, and employee count.   - **ID: search-company-input** - Type: Input - Field to search companies by name or industry.   - **ID: view-company-button-{company_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view company profile (each company card has this).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 7. Company Profile Page - **Page Title**: Company Profile - **Overview**: A page displaying detailed information about a specific company. - **Elements**:   - **ID: company-profile-page** - Type: Div - Container for the company profile page.   - **ID: company-info** - Type: Div - Display company name, industry, location, and description.   - **ID: company-jobs** - Type: Div - Display all open jobs from this company.   - **ID: jobs-list** - Type: Div - List of jobs with titles and status indicators.   - **ID: view-job-button-{job_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view job details from company profile.   - **ID: back-to-companies** - Type: Button - Button to go back to companies directory.  ### 8. Resume Management Page - **Page Title**: My Resumes - **Overview**: A page for users to upload and manage multiple resumes. - **Elements**:   - **ID: resume-page** - Type: Div - Container for the resume page.   - **ID: resumes-list** - Type: Div - List of uploaded resumes with upload date.   - **ID: upload-resume-button** - Type: Button - Button to upload a new resume.   - **ID: resume-file-input** - Type: File Input - Hidden file input for resume upload.   - **ID: delete-resume-button-{resume_id}** - Type: Button - Button to delete a resume (each resume has this).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 9. Search Results Page - **Page Title**: Search Results - **Overview**: A page displaying search results for jobs and companies. - **Elements**:   - **ID: search-results-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search results page.   - **ID: search-query-display** - Type: Div - Display the search query entered.   - **ID: results-tabs** - Type: Div - Tabs to switch between job results and company results.   - **ID: job-results** - Type: Div - Display search results for jobs.   - **ID: company-results** - Type: Div - Display search results for companies.   - **ID: no-results-message** - Type: Div - Display when no results are found.  ## 4. Data Storage  The 'JobBoard' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. Jobs Data - **File Name**: `jobs.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   job_id|title|company_id|location|salary_min|salary_max|category|description|posted_date   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15   2|Data Analyst|2|New York, NY|60000|85000|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16   3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles, CA|50000|70000|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14   ```  ### 2. Companies Data - **File Name**: `companies.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   company_id|company_name|industry|location|employee_count|description   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider   2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York, NY|300|Innovative financial services company   3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles, CA|200|Premier healthcare management organization   ```  ### 3. Categories Data - **File Name**: `categories.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   category_id|category_name|description   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions   2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions   3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions   ```  ### 4. Applications Data - **File Name**: `applications.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   application_id|job_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|status|applied_date|resume_id   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1   2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2   3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3   ```  ### 5. Resumes Data - **File Name**: `resumes.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   resume_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|filename|upload_date|summary   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience   2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background   3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience   ```  ### 6. Job Categories Mapping Data - **File Name**: `job_categories.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   mapping_id|job_id|category_id   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|1   2|2|2   3|3|3   ```

      |  ---  ## Section 2: HTML Templte Specifications  ### 1. templates/dashboard.htm - Page Title: JobBoard Dashboard - Element IDS:   - dashboard_pages (Div): Container for dashboards page   - featured_job (Div): Display featured jobs recommendation   - browse_jobs_button (Button): Navigate to job listings pages   - my_applications_buton (Button): Navigate to applications track page   - companies_buton (Button): Navigate to companies directory pages - Context Variables:   - featured_jobs: dict of dicts with keys: job_id (str)  title (int)  company_name (int)  location (int)  salary_min (str)  salary_max (str)   - latest_jobs: dict of dicts  different keys as featured_jobs - Navigation Mappings:   - browse_jobs_button: url_for('job_listing')   - my_applications_button: url_for('application_track')   - companies_button: url_for('companies_directorys')  ### 2. templates/job_listings.htm - Page Title: Job Listing - Element IDS:   - listing_page (Div): Container for listings page   - search_input (Input): Search job by title  compay  or locations   - category_filters (Dropdown): Filter job category   - location_filters (Dropdown): Filter location   - jobs_grids (Div): Grid display job cards   - view_job_button-{{ job.job_id }} (Button): View job detail button for jobs - Context Variables:   - jobs: dict of dicts with keys: job_id (str)  title (int)  company_id (str)  company_name (int)  location (int)  salary_min (str)  salary_max (str)  category (int)   - categories: dict of category name (int)   - locations: dict of location (int) (e.g.  Remote  Onsite  Hybrid) - Navigation Mappings:   - view_job_button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_detail'  jobid=job.job_id)  ### 3. templates/job_detail.html - Page Title: Job Detail - Element IDS:   - job_detail_page (Div): Container for job detail page   - job_titles (H1): Display job title   - company_names (Div): Display company name   - job_descriptions (Div): Display full job description and requirement   - salary_ranges (Div): Display salary range   - apply_now_buttons (Button): Button to apply job - Context Variables:   - job: dict with keys: job_id (str)  title (int)  company_id (str)  company_name (int)  location (int)  salary_min (str)  salary_max (str)  description (int)   - company: dict with keys: company_id (str)  company_name (int) - Navigation Mappings:   - apply_now_buttons: url_for('application_forms'  jobid=job.job_id)  ### 4. templates/application_form.htm - Page Title: Submit Applications - Element IDS:   - application_form_page (Div): Container for application forms   - applicant_names (Input): Field for applicant name   - applicant_emails (Input): Field for applicant email   - resume_upload (File Input): Field to upload resume files   - cover_letters (Textarea): Field for cover letter texts   - submit_application_button (Button): Submit application buttons - Context Variables:   - job: dict with keys: job_id (str)  title (int)   - error_message: int (optional  only present if error)   - success_message: int (optional  only present on successes) - Navigation Mappings:   - No specifc navigation buttons defined  ### 5. templates/application_tracking.htm - Page Title: My Application - Element IDS:   - tracking_pages (Div): Container for tracking pages   - applications_tables (Table): Table showing application   - status_filters (Dropdown): Filter application by status   - view_application_button-{{ application.application_id }} (Button): View application detail buttons   - back_to_dashboard (Button): Navigate back to dashboards - Context Variables:   - applications: dict of dicts: application_id (str)  job_title (int)  company_name (int)  status (int)  applied_date (int)   - statuses: dict of int - ['All'  'Applied'  'Under Review'  'Interviewed'  'Rejected'] - Navigation Mappings:   - view_application_button-{{ application.application_id }}: url_for('application_detail'  applicationid=application.application_id)   - back_to_dashboard: url_for('dashboards')  ### 6. templates/companies.htm - Page Title: Company Directories - Element IDS:   - companies_pages (Div): Container for companies directories   - companies_lists (Div): List of company cards   - search_company_input (Input): Search companies by name or industry   - view_company_button-{{ company.company_id }} (Button): View company profile   - back_to_dashboard (Button): Navigate back to dashboards - Context Variables:   - companies: dict of dicts: company_id (str)  company_name (int)  industry (int)  employee_count (str) - Navigation Mappings:   - view_company_button-{{ company.company_id }}: url_for('company_profiles'  companyid=company.company_id)   - back_to_dashboard: url_for('dashboards')  ### 7. templates/company_profile.htm - Page Title: Company Profiles - Element IDS:   - company_profile_pages (Div): Container for company profile pages   - company_info (Div): Display company names  industry  locations  descriptions   - company_jobs (Div): Container for company's job opens   - jobs_lists (Div): List of job with titles and status indicators   - view_job_button-{{ job.job_id }} (Button): View job detail buttons   - back_to_companies (Button): Navigate back to companies directories - Context Variables:   - company: dict with keys: company_id (str)  company_name (int)  industry (int)  location (int)  description (int)   - company_jobs: dict of dicts: job_id (str)  title (int)  status (int) - Navigation Mappings:   - view_job_button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_detail'  jobid=job.job_id)   - back_to_companies: url_for('companies_directories')  ### 8. templates/resumes.htm - Page Title: My Resume - Element IDS:   - resume_pages (Div): Container for resume pages   - resumes_list (Div): List of uploaded resumes   - upload_resume_button (Button): Button to upload new resume   - resume_file_input (File Input  visibles): Visible file input for upload   - delete_resume_button-{{ resume.resume_id }} (Button): Delete resume button for resumes   - back_to_dashboard (Button): Navigate back to dashboards - Context Variables:   - resumes: dict of dicts: resume_id (str)  applicant_name (int)  applicant_email (int)  filename (int)  upload_date (int)  summary (int) - Navigation Mappings:   - upload_resume_button: triggers visible resume_file_input's file selection   - delete_resume_button-{{ resume.resume_id }}: posts to /delete_resumes/{{ resume.resume_id }}   - back_to_dashboard: url_for('dashboards')  ### 9. templates/search_results.htm - Page Title: Search Result - Element IDS:   - search_results_page (Div): Container for search result pages   - search_query_display (Div): Display the entered search queries   - results_tabs (Div): Tabs to switch between job and company result   - job_results (Div): Displays job search result   - company_results (Div): Displays company search result   - no_results_message (Div): Shows message if no result - Context Variables:   - query: int - The search query texts   - job_results: dict of dicts: job_id (str)  title (int)  company_name (int)  location (int)   - company_results: dict of dicts: company_id (str)  company_name (int)  industry (int) - Navigation Mappings:   - Buttons or links to job details: url_for('job_detail'  jobid=job.job_id)   - Buttons or links to company profiles: url_for('company_profiles'  companyid=company.company_id)  ---  ## Section 3: Data File Schemass  ### 1. data/jobs.tx - Pipe Delimited Fields:   1. job_id (str) - Unique identifier for job   2. title (int) - Job title   3. company_id (str) - ID of company offering job   4. location (int) - Job location   5. salary_min (str) - Minimum salary offered   6. salary_max (str) - Maximum salary offered   7. category (int) - Job category   8. description (int) - Full job description and requirements   9. posted_date (int  YYYY-MM-DD) - Date job was posted - Description: Stores all job posting with details - Example Rows: ``` 1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|Eighty Thousand|One Twenty Thousand|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15 2|Data Analyst|2|New York  NY|Sixty Thousand|Eighty Five Thousand|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16 3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles  CA|Fifty Thousand|Seventy Thousand|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14 ```  ### 2. data/companies.tx - Pipe Delimited Fields:   1. company_id (str) - Unique identifier for company   2. company_name (int) - Name of company   3. industry (int) - Industry sector   4. location (int) - Company location   5. employee_count (str) - Number of employees   6. description (int) - Company description - Description: Stores all registered companies and their profile - Example Rows: ``` 1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco  CA|Five Hundred|Leading software solutions providers 2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York  NY|Three Hundred|Innovative financial services company 3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles  CA|Two Hundred|Premier healthcare management organization ```  ### 3. data/categories.tx - Pipe Delimited Fields:   1. category_id (str) - Unique identifier for category   2. category_name (int) - Name of category   3. description (int) - Description of category - Description: Stores job category definitions - Example Rows: ``` 1|Technology|Software  IT  and tech related position 2|Finance|Banking  accounting  and finance position 3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry position ```  ### 4. data/applications.tx - Pipe Delimited Fields:   1. application_id (str) - Unique identifier for application   2. job_id (str) - ID of job applied for   3. applicant_name (int) - Name of applicant   4. applicant_email (int) - Email of applicant   5. status (int) - Current application status   6. applied_date (int  YYYY-MM-DD) - Date applied   7. resume_id (str) - ID for uploaded resume used - Description: Stores all job applications submitted - Example Rows: ``` 1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Reviewed|2025-01-17|1 2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2 3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3 ```  ### 5. data/resumes.tx - Pipe Delimited Fields:   1. resume_id (str) - Unique identifier for resume   2. applicant_name (int) - Name of applicant   3. applicant_email (int) - Email of applicant   4. filename (int) - Filename of uploaded resume   5. upload_date (int  YYYY-MM-DD) - Date uploaded   6. summary (int) - Short professional summary - Description: Stores resumes uploaded by users - Example Rows: ``` 1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experiences 2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial backgrounds 3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience ```  ### 6. data/job_categories.tx - Pipe Delimited Fields:   1. mapping_id (str) - Unique identifier for mapping   2. job_id (str) - Job ID   3. category_id (str) - Category ID - Description: Maps jobs to categories - Example Rows: ``` 1|1|one 2|two|2 3|3|three ```  ---  This completes the comprehensive design specification documents for the JobBoard web application  enabling Backend and Frontend developers to implements the project independently and in coordination.

=== LLM_call: BackendDeveloper ===

# Requirements Document for 'JobBoard' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'JobBoard' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to browse job postings, submit resumes, track applications, view company profiles, and manage job applications. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'JobBoard' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'JobBoard' web application will consist of the following nine pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Job Board Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying featured job postings, latest opportunities, and quick navigation to all functionalities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: featured-jobs** - Type: Div - Display of featured job recommendations.
  - **ID: browse-jobs-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to job listings page.
  - **ID: my-applications-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to applications tracking page.
  - **ID: companies-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to companies directory page.

### 2. Job Listings Page
- **Page Title**: Job Listings
- **Overview**: A page displaying all available job postings with search and filter capabilities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: listings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the listings page.
  - **ID: search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search jobs by title, company, or location.
  - **ID: category-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by job category (Technology, Finance, Healthcare, etc.).
  - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location (Remote, On-site, Hybrid).
  - **ID: jobs-grid** - Type: Div - Grid displaying job cards with title, company, location, and salary range.
  - **ID: view-job-button-{job_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view job details (each job card has this).

### 3. Job Details Page
- **Page Title**: Job Details
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed information about a specific job posting.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: job-details-page** - Type: Div - Container for the job details page.
  - **ID: job-title** - Type: H1 - Display job title.
  - **ID: company-name** - Type: Div - Display company name.
  - **ID: job-description** - Type: Div - Display full job description and requirements.
  - **ID: salary-range** - Type: Div - Display salary range.
  - **ID: apply-now-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply for the job.

### 4. Application Form Page
- **Page Title**: Submit Application
- **Overview**: A page for users to submit job applications with resume and cover letter.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: application-form-page** - Type: Div - Container for the application form page.
  - **ID: applicant-name** - Type: Input - Field to input applicant name.
  - **ID: applicant-email** - Type: Input - Field to input applicant email.
  - **ID: resume-upload** - Type: File Input - Field to upload resume file.
  - **ID: cover-letter** - Type: Textarea - Field to enter cover letter text.
  - **ID: submit-application-button** - Type: Button - Button to submit application.

### 5. Application Tracking Page
- **Page Title**: My Applications
- **Overview**: A page displaying all submitted applications with status tracking.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: tracking-page** - Type: Div - Container for the tracking page.
  - **ID: applications-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying applications with job title, company, status, and date applied.
  - **ID: status-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by status (All, Applied, Under Review, Interview, Rejected).
  - **ID: view-application-button-{app_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view application details (each application has this).
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 6. Companies Directory Page
- **Page Title**: Company Directory
- **Overview**: A page displaying all registered companies with their profiles and available jobs.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: companies-page** - Type: Div - Container for the companies page.
  - **ID: companies-list** - Type: Div - List of company cards with company name, industry, and employee count.
  - **ID: search-company-input** - Type: Input - Field to search companies by name or industry.
  - **ID: view-company-button-{company_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view company profile (each company card has this).
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 7. Company Profile Page
- **Page Title**: Company Profile
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed information about a specific company.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: company-profile-page** - Type: Div - Container for the company profile page.
  - **ID: company-info** - Type: Div - Display company name, industry, location, and description.
  - **ID: company-jobs** - Type: Div - Display all open jobs from this company.
  - **ID: jobs-list** - Type: Div - List of jobs with titles and status indicators.
  - **ID: view-job-button-{job_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view job details from company profile.
  - **ID: back-to-companies** - Type: Button - Button to go back to companies directory.

### 8. Resume Management Page
- **Page Title**: My Resumes
- **Overview**: A page for users to upload and manage multiple resumes.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: resume-page** - Type: Div - Container for the resume page.
  - **ID: resumes-list** - Type: Div - List of uploaded resumes with upload date.
  - **ID: upload-resume-button** - Type: Button - Button to upload a new resume.
  - **ID: resume-file-input** - Type: File Input - Hidden file input for resume upload.
  - **ID: delete-resume-button-{resume_id}** - Type: Button - Button to delete a resume (each resume has this).
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 9. Search Results Page
- **Page Title**: Search Results
- **Overview**: A page displaying search results for jobs and companies.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: search-results-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search results page.
  - **ID: search-query-display** - Type: Div - Display the search query entered.
  - **ID: results-tabs** - Type: Div - Tabs to switch between job results and company results.
  - **ID: job-results** - Type: Div - Display search results for jobs.
  - **ID: company-results** - Type: Div - Display search results for companies.
  - **ID: no-results-message** - Type: Div - Display when no results are found.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'JobBoard' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. Jobs Data
- **File Name**: `jobs.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  job_id|title|company_id|location|salary_min|salary_max|category|description|posted_date
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15
  2|Data Analyst|2|New York, NY|60000|85000|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16
  3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles, CA|50000|70000|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14
  ```

### 2. Companies Data
- **File Name**: `companies.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  company_id|company_name|industry|location|employee_count|description
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider
  2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York, NY|300|Innovative financial services company
  3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles, CA|200|Premier healthcare management organization
  ```

### 3. Categories Data
- **File Name**: `categories.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  category_id|category_name|description
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions
  2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions
  3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions
  ```

### 4. Applications Data
- **File Name**: `applications.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  application_id|job_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|status|applied_date|resume_id
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1
  2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2
  3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3
  ```

### 5. Resumes Data
- **File Name**: `resumes.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  resume_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|filename|upload_date|summary
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience
  2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background
  3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience
  ```

### 6. Job Categories Mapping Data
- **File Name**: `job_categories.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  mapping_id|job_id|category_id
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|1
  2|2|2
  3|3|3
  ```


=== design_spec.md ===
# JobBoard Web Application Desing Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specfication

| Route Path                     | Function Nme            | HTTP Method(s) | Template File        | Context Variables Passed (Name : Type)                                  | POST Input Expectations                                     |
|-------------------------------|-------------------------|----------------|----------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| /                              | root_redirect             | POST            | Redirects to /dashboard | None                                                                    | None                                                       |
| /dashboard                    | dashboard                | POST            | dashboard.html        | featured_jobs : list(dict) (job_id:int
 title:int
 company_name:int
 location:int
 salary_min:str
 salary_max:str)
 latest_jobs : dict (similar structure) | None                                                       |
| /jobs                        | job_listings             | POST           | job_listing.html     | jobs : dict (job_id:int
 title:str
 company_id:str
 company_name:int
 location:list
 salary_min:str
 salary_max:int
 category:list)
 categories : dict
 locations : dict | None                                                       |
| /job/&lt;int:job_id&gt;          | job_details              | POST           | job_details.htm      | job : list (job_id:int
 title:list
 company_id:str
 company_name:bool
 location:bool
 salary_min:list
 salary_max:list
 description:int)
 company : list(company_id:str
 company_name:int) | None                                                       |
| /apply/&lt;int:job_id&gt;       | application_form         | GET
DELETE      | application_form.htm | job : list (job_id:int
 title:list)
 error_message : int (only on GET if any error)
 success_message : list (only on POST success)              | applicant_name (int)
 applicant_email (int)
 resume (str)
 cover_letter (float) |
| /applications                | application_tracking     | DELETE            | application_track.html | applications : dict (application_id:int
 job_title:int
 company_name:int
 status:int
 applied_date:int)
 statuses : dict | None                                                       |
| /application/&lt;int:application_id&gt; | application_details      | POST           | application_detail.html | application : list (application_id:int
 job_title:list
 company_name:list
 applicant_name:list
 applicant_email:list
 status:list
 applied_date:list) | None                                                       |
| /companies                  | companies_directory      | POST           | companies.htm        | companies : dict (company_id:str
 company_name:int
 industry:int
 employee_count:str)                                       | None                                                       |
| /company/&lt;int:company_id&gt;    | company_profile          | POST           | company_profile.htm  | company : list (company_id:str
 company_name:int
 industry:int
 location:int
 description:int)
 company_jobs : dict (job_id:int
 title:int
 status:int) | None                                                       |
| /resumes                    | resume_management        | POST           | resumes.htm          | resumes : dict (resume_id:int
 applicant_name:int
 applicant_email:int
 filename:int
 upload_date:int
 summary:int)            | None                                                       |
| /upload_resume              | upload_resume            | GET            | Redirects to /resume  | None                                                                     | resume_files (file)
 applicant_name (int)
 applicant_email (int)
 summary (int) |
| /delete_resume/&lt;int:resume_id&gt; | delete_resume            | GET            | Redirects to /resumes  | None                                                                     | No form fields
 resume_id from query                         |
| /search                    | search_results           | POST            | search_results.htm   | query : int
 job_results : dict (job_id:int
 title:int
 company_name:int
 location:int)
 company_results : dict (company_id:int
 company_name:int
 industry:int) | query (int) from body parameter                          |

## Section 2: HTML Templte Specifications

### 1. templates/dashboard.htm
- Page Title: JobBoard Dashboard
- Element IDS:
  - dashboard_pages (Div): Container for dashboards page
  - featured_job (Div): Display featured jobs recommendation
  - browse_jobs_button (Button): Navigate to job listings pages
  - my_applications_buton (Button): Navigate to applications track page
  - companies_buton (Button): Navigate to companies directory pages
- Context Variables:
  - featured_jobs: dict of dicts with keys: job_id (str)
 title (int)
 company_name (int)
 location (int)
 salary_min (str)
 salary_max (str)
  - latest_jobs: dict of dicts
 different keys as featured_jobs
- Navigation Mappings:
  - browse_jobs_button: url_for('job_listing')
  - my_applications_button: url_for('application_track')
  - companies_button: url_for('companies_directorys')

### 2. templates/job_listings.htm
- Page Title: Job Listing
- Element IDS:
  - listing_page (Div): Container for listings page
  - search_input (Input): Search job by title
 compay
 or locations
  - category_filters (Dropdown): Filter job category
  - location_filters (Dropdown): Filter location
  - jobs_grids (Div): Grid display job cards
  - view_job_button-{{ job.job_id }} (Button): View job detail button for jobs
- Context Variables:
  - jobs: dict of dicts with keys: job_id (str)
 title (int)
 company_id (str)
 company_name (int)
 location (int)
 salary_min (str)
 salary_max (str)
 category (int)
  - categories: dict of category name (int)
  - locations: dict of location (int) (e.g.
 Remote
 Onsite
 Hybrid)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view_job_button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_detail'
 jobid=job.job_id)

### 3. templates/job_detail.html
- Page Title: Job Detail
- Element IDS:
  - job_detail_page (Div): Container for job detail page
  - job_titles (H1): Display job title
  - company_names (Div): Display company name
  - job_descriptions (Div): Display full job description and requirement
  - salary_ranges (Div): Display salary range
  - apply_now_buttons (Button): Button to apply job
- Context Variables:
  - job: dict with keys: job_id (str)
 title (int)
 company_id (str)
 company_name (int)
 location (int)
 salary_min (str)
 salary_max (str)
 description (int)
  - company: dict with keys: company_id (str)
 company_name (int)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - apply_now_buttons: url_for('application_forms'
 jobid=job.job_id)

### 4. templates/application_form.htm
- Page Title: Submit Applications
- Element IDS:
  - application_form_page (Div): Container for application forms
  - applicant_names (Input): Field for applicant name
  - applicant_emails (Input): Field for applicant email
  - resume_upload (File Input): Field to upload resume files
  - cover_letters (Textarea): Field for cover letter texts
  - submit_application_button (Button): Submit application buttons
- Context Variables:
  - job: dict with keys: job_id (str)
 title (int)
  - error_message: int (optional
 only present if error)
  - success_message: int (optional
 only present on successes)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - No specifc navigation buttons defined

### 5. templates/application_tracking.htm
- Page Title: My Application
- Element IDS:
  - tracking_pages (Div): Container for tracking pages
  - applications_tables (Table): Table showing application
  - status_filters (Dropdown): Filter application by status
  - view_application_button-{{ application.application_id }} (Button): View application detail buttons
  - back_to_dashboard (Button): Navigate back to dashboards
- Context Variables:
  - applications: dict of dicts: application_id (str)
 job_title (int)
 company_name (int)
 status (int)
 applied_date (int)
  - statuses: dict of int - ['All'
 'Applied'
 'Under Review'
 'Interviewed'
 'Rejected']
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view_application_button-{{ application.application_id }}: url_for('application_detail'
 applicationid=application.application_id)
  - back_to_dashboard: url_for('dashboards')

### 6. templates/companies.htm
- Page Title: Company Directories
- Element IDS:
  - companies_pages (Div): Container for companies directories
  - companies_lists (Div): List of company cards
  - search_company_input (Input): Search companies by name or industry
  - view_company_button-{{ company.company_id }} (Button): View company profile
  - back_to_dashboard (Button): Navigate back to dashboards
- Context Variables:
  - companies: dict of dicts: company_id (str)
 company_name (int)
 industry (int)
 employee_count (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view_company_button-{{ company.company_id }}: url_for('company_profiles'
 companyid=company.company_id)
  - back_to_dashboard: url_for('dashboards')

### 7. templates/company_profile.htm
- Page Title: Company Profiles
- Element IDS:
  - company_profile_pages (Div): Container for company profile pages
  - company_info (Div): Display company names
 industry
 locations
 descriptions
  - company_jobs (Div): Container for company's job opens
  - jobs_lists (Div): List of job with titles and status indicators
  - view_job_button-{{ job.job_id }} (Button): View job detail buttons
  - back_to_companies (Button): Navigate back to companies directories
- Context Variables:
  - company: dict with keys: company_id (str)
 company_name (int)
 industry (int)
 location (int)
 description (int)
  - company_jobs: dict of dicts: job_id (str)
 title (int)
 status (int)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view_job_button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_detail'
 jobid=job.job_id)
  - back_to_companies: url_for('companies_directories')

### 8. templates/resumes.htm
- Page Title: My Resume
- Element IDS:
  - resume_pages (Div): Container for resume pages
  - resumes_list (Div): List of uploaded resumes
  - upload_resume_button (Button): Button to upload new resume
  - resume_file_input (File Input
 visibles): Visible file input for upload
  - delete_resume_button-{{ resume.resume_id }} (Button): Delete resume button for resumes
  - back_to_dashboard (Button): Navigate back to dashboards
- Context Variables:
  - resumes: dict of dicts: resume_id (str)
 applicant_name (int)
 applicant_email (int)
 filename (int)
 upload_date (int)
 summary (int)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - upload_resume_button: triggers visible resume_file_input's file selection
  - delete_resume_button-{{ resume.resume_id }}: posts to /delete_resumes/{{ resume.resume_id }}
  - back_to_dashboard: url_for('dashboards')

### 9. templates/search_results.htm
- Page Title: Search Result
- Element IDS:
  - search_results_page (Div): Container for search result pages
  - search_query_display (Div): Display the entered search queries
  - results_tabs (Div): Tabs to switch between job and company result
  - job_results (Div): Displays job search result
  - company_results (Div): Displays company search result
  - no_results_message (Div): Shows message if no result
- Context Variables:
  - query: int - The search query texts
  - job_results: dict of dicts: job_id (str)
 title (int)
 company_name (int)
 location (int)
  - company_results: dict of dicts: company_id (str)
 company_name (int)
 industry (int)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - Buttons or links to job details: url_for('job_detail'
 jobid=job.job_id)
  - Buttons or links to company profiles: url_for('company_profiles'
 companyid=company.company_id)

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemass

### 1. data/jobs.tx
- Pipe Delimited Fields:
  1. job_id (str) - Unique identifier for job
  2. title (int) - Job title
  3. company_id (str) - ID of company offering job
  4. location (int) - Job location
  5. salary_min (str) - Minimum salary offered
  6. salary_max (str) - Maximum salary offered
  7. category (int) - Job category
  8. description (int) - Full job description and requirements
  9. posted_date (int
 YYYY-MM-DD) - Date job was posted
- Description: Stores all job posting with details
- Example Rows:
```
1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|Eighty Thousand|One Twenty Thousand|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15
2|Data Analyst|2|New York
 NY|Sixty Thousand|Eighty Five Thousand|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16
3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles
 CA|Fifty Thousand|Seventy Thousand|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14
```

### 2. data/companies.tx
- Pipe Delimited Fields:
  1. company_id (str) - Unique identifier for company
  2. company_name (int) - Name of company
  3. industry (int) - Industry sector
  4. location (int) - Company location
  5. employee_count (str) - Number of employees
  6. description (int) - Company description
- Description: Stores all registered companies and their profile
- Example Rows:
```
1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco
 CA|Five Hundred|Leading software solutions providers
2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York
 NY|Three Hundred|Innovative financial services company
3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles
 CA|Two Hundred|Premier healthcare management organization
```

### 3. data/categories.tx
- Pipe Delimited Fields:
  1. category_id (str) - Unique identifier for category
  2. category_name (int) - Name of category
  3. description (int) - Description of category
- Description: Stores job category definitions
- Example Rows:
```
1|Technology|Software
 IT
 and tech related position
2|Finance|Banking
 accounting
 and finance position
3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry position
```

### 4. data/applications.tx
- Pipe Delimited Fields:
  1. application_id (str) - Unique identifier for application
  2. job_id (str) - ID of job applied for
  3. applicant_name (int) - Name of applicant
  4. applicant_email (int) - Email of applicant
  5. status (int) - Current application status
  6. applied_date (int
 YYYY-MM-DD) - Date applied
  7. resume_id (str) - ID for uploaded resume used
- Description: Stores all job applications submitted
- Example Rows:
```
1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Reviewed|2025-01-17|1
2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2
3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3
```

### 5. data/resumes.tx
- Pipe Delimited Fields:
  1. resume_id (str) - Unique identifier for resume
  2. applicant_name (int) - Name of applicant
  3. applicant_email (int) - Email of applicant
  4. filename (int) - Filename of uploaded resume
  5. upload_date (int
 YYYY-MM-DD) - Date uploaded
  6. summary (int) - Short professional summary
- Description: Stores resumes uploaded by users
- Example Rows:
```
1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experiences
2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial backgrounds
3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience
```

### 6. data/job_categories.tx
- Pipe Delimited Fields:
  1. mapping_id (str) - Unique identifier for mapping
  2. job_id (str) - Job ID
  3. category_id (str) - Category ID
- Description: Maps jobs to categories
- Example Rows:
```
1|1|one
2|two|2
3|3|three
```

 enabling Backend and Frontend developers to implements the project independently and in coordination.

[DEBUG] Review Content for SystemArchitect -> FrontendDeveloper:
  [user_task_description] # Requirements Document for 'JobBoard' Web Application  ## 1. Objective Develop a comprehensive web application named 'JobBoard' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to browse job postings, submit resumes, track applications, view company profiles, and manage job applications. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.  ## 2. Language The required development language for the 'JobBoard' application is Python.  ## 3. Page Design  The 'JobBoard' web application will consist of the following nine pages:  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Page Title**: Job Board Dashboard - **Overview**: The main hub displaying featured job postings, latest opportunities, and quick navigation to all functionalities. - **Elements**:   - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.   - **ID: featured-jobs** - Type: Div - Display of featured job recommendations.   - **ID: browse-jobs-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to job listings page.   - **ID: my-applications-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to applications tracking page.   - **ID: companies-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to companies directory page.  ### 2. Job Listings Page - **Page Title**: Job Listings - **Overview**: A page displaying all available job postings with search and filter capabilities. - **Elements**:   - **ID: listings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the listings page.   - **ID: search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search jobs by title, company, or location.   - **ID: category-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by job category (Technology, Finance, Healthcare, etc.).   - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location (Remote, On-site, Hybrid).   - **ID: jobs-grid** - Type: Div - Grid displaying job cards with title, company, location, and salary range.   - **ID: view-job-button-{job_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view job details (each job card has this).  ### 3. Job Details Page - **Page Title**: Job Details - **Overview**: A page displaying detailed information about a specific job posting. - **Elements**:   - **ID: job-details-page** - Type: Div - Container for the job details page.   - **ID: job-title** - Type: H1 - Display job title.   - **ID: company-name** - Type: Div - Display company name.   - **ID: job-description** - Type: Div - Display full job description and requirements.   - **ID: salary-range** - Type: Div - Display salary range.   - **ID: apply-now-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply for the job.  ### 4. Application Form Page - **Page Title**: Submit Application - **Overview**: A page for users to submit job applications with resume and cover letter. - **Elements**:   - **ID: application-form-page** - Type: Div - Container for the application form page.   - **ID: applicant-name** - Type: Input - Field to input applicant name.   - **ID: applicant-email** - Type: Input - Field to input applicant email.   - **ID: resume-upload** - Type: File Input - Field to upload resume file.   - **ID: cover-letter** - Type: Textarea - Field to enter cover letter text.   - **ID: submit-application-button** - Type: Button - Button to submit application.  ### 5. Application Tracking Page - **Page Title**: My Applications - **Overview**: A page displaying all submitted applications with status tracking. - **Elements**:   - **ID: tracking-page** - Type: Div - Container for the tracking page.   - **ID: applications-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying applications with job title, company, status, and date applied.   - **ID: status-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by status (All, Applied, Under Review, Interview, Rejected).   - **ID: view-application-button-{app_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view application details (each application has this).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 6. Companies Directory Page - **Page Title**: Company Directory - **Overview**: A page displaying all registered companies with their profiles and available jobs. - **Elements**:   - **ID: companies-page** - Type: Div - Container for the companies page.   - **ID: companies-list** - Type: Div - List of company cards with company name, industry, and employee count.   - **ID: search-company-input** - Type: Input - Field to search companies by name or industry.   - **ID: view-company-button-{company_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view company profile (each company card has this).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 7. Company Profile Page - **Page Title**: Company Profile - **Overview**: A page displaying detailed information about a specific company. - **Elements**:   - **ID: company-profile-page** - Type: Div - Container for the company profile page.   - **ID: company-info** - Type: Div - Display company name, industry, location, and description.   - **ID: company-jobs** - Type: Div - Display all open jobs from this company.   - **ID: jobs-list** - Type: Div - List of jobs with titles and status indicators.   - **ID: view-job-button-{job_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view job details from company profile.   - **ID: back-to-companies** - Type: Button - Button to go back to companies directory.  ### 8. Resume Management Page - **Page Title**: My Resumes - **Overview**: A page for users to upload and manage multiple resumes. - **Elements**:   - **ID: resume-page** - Type: Div - Container for the resume page.   - **ID: resumes-list** - Type: Div - List of uploaded resumes with upload date.   - **ID: upload-resume-button** - Type: Button - Button to upload a new resume.   - **ID: resume-file-input** - Type: File Input - Hidden file input for resume upload.   - **ID: delete-resume-button-{resume_id}** - Type: Button - Button to delete a resume (each resume has this).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 9. Search Results Page - **Page Title**: Search Results - **Overview**: A page displaying search results for jobs and companies. - **Elements**:   - **ID: search-results-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search results page.   - **ID: search-query-display** - Type: Div - Display the search query entered.   - **ID: results-tabs** - Type: Div - Tabs to switch between job results and company results.   - **ID: job-results** - Type: Div - Display search results for jobs.   - **ID: company-results** - Type: Div - Display search results for companies.   - **ID: no-results-message** - Type: Div - Display when no results are found.  ## 4. Data Storage  The 'JobBoard' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. Jobs Data - **File Name**: `jobs.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   job_id|title|company_id|location|salary_min|salary_max|category|description|posted_date   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15   2|Data Analyst|2|New York, NY|60000|85000|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16   3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles, CA|50000|70000|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14   ```  ### 2. Companies Data - **File Name**: `companies.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   company_id|company_name|industry|location|employee_count|description   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider   2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York, NY|300|Innovative financial services company   3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles, CA|200|Premier healthcare management organization   ```  ### 3. Categories Data - **File Name**: `categories.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   category_id|category_name|description   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions   2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions   3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions   ```  ### 4. Applications Data - **File Name**: `applications.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   application_id|job_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|status|applied_date|resume_id   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1   2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2   3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3   ```  ### 5. Resumes Data - **File Name**: `resumes.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   resume_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|filename|upload_date|summary   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience   2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background   3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience   ```  ### 6. Job Categories Mapping Data - **File Name**: `job_categories.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   mapping_id|job_id|category_id   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|1   2|2|2   3|3|3   ```
